title: Microtask
body:
## Definition

A **microtask** is a short async job queued after the current script and before the next render/macrotask — Promise reactions, `queueMicrotask`, `MutationObserver`.

## Why it exists

Promises need predictable “run soon” ordering that beats timers and I/O, so `.then` runs before `setTimeout(0)`.

## How it works

When a Promise settles, handlers are queued as microtasks. After synchronous code finishes, the engine drains **all** microtasks in the queue before taking the next [macrotask](concept:macrotask) from the [event loop](concept:event-loop).

## Example

```js
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log("micro"));
setTimeout(() => console.log("macro"), 0);
```

## Common confusion

Microtasks can starve macrotasks if you chain infinite `then` — the loop never reaches timers. Avoid recursive Promise chains without yielding.

## In practice

Explains `Promise.resolve().then` vs `setTimeout` ordering in interviews and React 18 batching interactions with microtasks.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

A **[microtask](concept:microtask)** is a short [async](concept:async-await) job queued after the current script and before the next render/macrotask — [Promise](concept:promise) reactions, `queueMicrotask`, [MutationObserver](concept:mutation-observer).

## Why it exists

[Promises](concept:promise) need predictable “run soon” ordering that beats [timers](concept:timers) and I/O, so `.then` runs before [setTimeout(0)](concept:timers).

## How it works

When a [Promise](concept:promise) settles, handlers are queued as [microtasks](concept:microtask). After synchronous code finishes, the engine drains **all** [microtasks](concept:microtask) in the queue before taking the next [macrotask](concept:macrotask) from the [event loop](concept:event-loop).

## Example

```js
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log("micro"));
setTimeout(() => console.log("macro"), 0);
```

## Common confusion

[Microtasks](concept:microtask) can starve [macrotasks](concept:macrotask) if you chain infinite `then` — the loop never reaches [timers](concept:timers). Avoid recursive [Promise](concept:promise) chains without yielding.

## In practice

Explains `Promise.resolve().then` vs [setTimeout](concept:timers) ordering in interviews and [React 18 batching](concept:react-batching) interactions with [microtasks](concept:microtask).
